title: Podman warns about issues with CNI, even though CNI isnt active
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman reports warning in regards to CNI, even if only Netavark is used

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman and netavark, dont install / remove all CNI tools

2. run `podman network ls` (as root)

**Describe the results you received:**

I get a warning about CNI, even though I dont use it:

```
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist: [failed to find plugin "bridge" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "tuning" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]] 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

No warning

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.19
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


No

comments.body:
This is expected and not a bug.

On the first run we have to check if podman was used previously in which case we default to cni. To do so we will check for cni configs and possible detect errors and warn about that.
Assuming you did not create the cni config and it was shipped by your distro I would say this is packing bug. At least since podman v3.2 there shouldn't be a default config shipped since podman will create it automatically.
